statement.en: Interference RNA and microRNA in Rheumatoid Arthritis: Diagnostic Biomarkers, Epigenetic Regulation, and Emerging Therapeutic Strategies
statement.summary: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a persistent autoimmune disorder where traditional serological indicators, including rheumatoid factor and anti-citrullinated protein antibodies, are inadequate for early and precise diagnosis, and existing anti-cytokine treatments have effectiveness and safety constraints. Recent data indicates that non-coding microRNAs (miRNAs) play a crucial role in the epigenetic control of inflammatory and catabolic pathways in the synovium, cartilage, and circulating immune cells, therefore facilitating the development and progression of RA. This review delineates the diagnostic efficacy of circulating and cell-associated miRNAs, specifically miR-16, miR-132, miR-146a, miR-155, and miR-223, which exhibit aberrant expression in peripheral blood mononuclear cells and T-lymp

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom presents a literature review of emerging diagnostic and therapeutic strategies in miRNA-based RA biomarkers. The specific miRNA candidates (miR-16, miR-132, miR-146a, miR-155, miR-223) and their diagnostic efficacy are under active scientific validation; clinical translation remains provisional and validation-dependent. The work is dated 2026 (future date in evaluation context) and categorized as cs.AI, suggesting computational/ML integration not yet standardized in clinical practice.
